title: Xanthous Waxy Cantonese Melons Century Egg Congee
description: Winey and bouncy, this midnight-craving Chinese (Cantonese) dish features sweet melons and savory century egg in a comforting congee.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat. Add 2 cloves of minced garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the century eggs and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to break apart, about 2 minutes.
Add the Xanthous Waxy Melons and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the rice and stir to combine with the vegetables and eggs.
Add 4 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the congee is creamy and the rice is broken down, about 20 minutes.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the congee hot, garnished with additional Xanthous Waxy Melon slices if desired.
